fundamentals.name: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. engages in the design, manufacture, and sale of fiber-optic networking products in the United States, Taiwan, and China. The company offers optical modules, optical filters, lasers, laser components, subassemblies, transmitters and transceivers, turn-key equipment, headend, node, and distribution equipment, as well as amplifiers. It sells its products to internet data center operators, cable television, telecom equipment manufacturers, fiber-to-the-home, and internet service providers through its direct and indirect sales channels. Applied Optoelectronics, Inc. was incorporated in 1997 and is headquartered in Sugar Land, Texas.

judgment.summary: Rapidly growing revenue and strong future growth estimates are offset by current unprofitability and a high valuation multiple.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter missed EPS estimates; next earnings date 2026-08-06.
positives: Revenue growth 51.4% YoY — indicating strong demand.
Analyst growth est, current FY 441.7% — exceptionally high future growth expectations.
Forward P/E 37.90 with PEG ratio 0.78 — suggests growth may justify the multiple.
Current ratio 3.83 and Quick ratio 2.85 — strong short-term liquidity.
Total cash $439.70M vs Total debt $280.42M — manageable debt levels.
negatives: Operating margin -8.6% and Profit margin -8.5% — the company is currently unprofitable.
Return on equity -6.1% and Return on assets -3.3% — negative returns on capital.
Free cash flow (TTM) $-445.58M — significant cash burn.
Beta 3.76 — stock exhibits high volatility relative to the market.

news.paragraph: Applied Optoelectronics (AAOI) has seen significant positive momentum, with the stock up 635.5% in six months, driven by large transceiver orders and a shifting business narrative. While the company's latest reported quarter missed EPS estimates, analyst growth estimates for the current quarter and fiscal year remain exceptionally high. The stock's recent surge and substantial analyst growth projections suggest strong investor interest, though the next earnings date on August 6th will be key for validation.
